nodes.label: phase8/ README — the economy: when do we pay for direction (the gate)
nodes.rationale: The unbuilt Ch7 learning-gate decides when the cheap path has stalled and a precise GD pass is worth its charge — a 20-year-old solved streaming problem (concept-drift detection), but false-fires burn the 80/20 so it's a problem to tune, not a free answer. Also builds the hardware-meaningful cost meter Stage 1 punted.

nodes.label: DDM / ADWIN drift-detection gate
nodes.rationale: DDM (error-rate, two thresholds: warning->buffer, drift->spend GD->replay-since-warning) IS the project's two-threshold gate + sleep; ADWIN (two-window mean test) IS the fast-EMA-vs-slow-EMA plateau detector, moving the magic number to an interpretable false-positive rate delta.

nodes.label: Firing the gate on SCFF features (ADWIN-U, label-free)
nodes.rationale: A label-free drift detector on the SCFF taps rather than on readout error, because SCFF drift leads the readout's error — schedules a refit before accuracy drops; the gate and the sleep-cadence are the same detector at two timescales.

nodes.label: Synthetic gradients / DNI — the cautionary don't
nodes.rationale: A predicted supervised gradient upstream of SCFF is exactly the forward-leak poison the project's constraints forbid — decoupled local updates degrade on depth; named as a negative control, not a candidate.

nodes.label: The cost meter (charge cycles / ADC conversions / write energy)
nodes.rationale: A required deliverable, not a research goal: every 'cheaper-than-backprop / 80-20' claim is ultimately a cost claim the op-count proxy can't settle — replaces the proxy with hardware-meaningful units.
